Anatomix-injection ablation

center-wise validation split · 207 subjects · all at the 100k-step (epoch 200) parity checkpoint · Track-A metrics + Hard Dice, identical definitions to full_eval.

Variants

  • unet-dice (no anatomix) (nbn71048_ep200): plain U-Net · L1 + SSIM + Dice · anatomix used nowhere, n=207
  • unet-perc (anatomix loss) (y5tqp2bt_ep200): plain U-Net · L1 + anatomix-NCC perceptual(0.1) + Dice · SSIM off · anatomix in the loss, n=207
  • amix v1.4 (anatomix backbone) (827la6dp_ep200): anatomix-feature translator · L1 + SSIM + Dice (perceptual_w=0) · anatomix in the architecture, n=207
What this ablation isolates. All three share the L1 + Dice base and the same center-wise data/budget (ep200 ≈ 100k steps), and differ only in where anatomix is injected: nowhere (unet-dice) → as a training loss (unet-perc, NCC on frozen anatomix decoder features) → as the model backbone (amix, a translator that operates on frozen anatomix features, no perceptual loss).
Caveat: unet-dice→unet-perc swaps SSIM for the perceptual term (repo rule: perceptual on ⇒ ssim_w=0), so that step is a substitution, not a pure addition; amix keeps SSIM. Each variant is a separate training run, so a small part of any gap is run-to-run noise — the per-subject Wilcoxon below tests whether the body-MAE difference is consistent across subjects.

Paired Wilcoxon — per-subject body MAE

Shared subjects only. p<0.05 ⇒ the two variants differ significantly; p≥0.05 ⇒ within noise.

unet-dice (no anatomix) unet-perc (anatomix loss) amix v1.4 (anatomix backbone)
unet-dice (no anatomix) — p=8.2e-20 p=6.4e-25

amix v1.4 (anatomix backbone) p=6.4e-25 p=3.4e-05 —
